q: In triangle XYZ, angle X = 35°, angle Y = 75°, and side y = 12 cm. Find the length of side x, correct to 3 significant figures.
e: Use the sine rule: $x/\sin(X) = y/\sin(Y)$. Rearranging gives $x = y \times \sin(X) / \sin(Y)$. So, $x = 12 \times \sin(35°) / \sin(75°) = 12 \times 0.5735... / 0.9659... = 7.1257...$ cm, which is 7.13 cm to 3 s.f.

q: In triangle PQR, angle P = 42°, angle Q = 58°, and side q = 15 m. Find the length of side p, correct to 3 significant figures.
e: Use the sine rule: $p/\sin(P) = q/\sin(Q)$. Rearranging gives $p = q \times \sin(P) / \sin(Q)$. So, $p = 15 \times \sin(42°) / \sin(58°) = 15 \times 0.6691... / 0.8480... = 11.835...$ m, which is 11.8 m to 3 s.f.

q: In triangle DEF, angle D = 50°, angle E = 70°, and side e = 8.5 cm. Find the length of side d, correct to 3 significant figures.
e: Use the sine rule: $d/\sin(D) = e/\sin(E)$. Rearranging gives $d = e \times \sin(D) / \sin(E)$. So, $d = 8.5 \times \sin(50°) / \sin(70°) = 8.5 \times 0.7660... / 0.9396... = 6.928...$ cm, which is 6.93 cm to 3 s.f.

q: In triangle ABC, side a = 7 cm, side b = 9 cm, and angle B = 65°. Find the size of angle A, correct to 1 decimal place.
e: Use the sine rule: $\sin(A)/a = \sin(B)/b$. Rearranging gives $\sin(A) = a \times \sin(B) / b$. So, $\sin(A) = 7 \times \sin(65°) / 9 = 0.7049...$ Angle $A = \arcsin(0.7049...) = 44.82...°$, which is 44.8° to 1 d.p.

q: In triangle LMN, side l = 12 m, side m = 18 m, and angle M = 110°. Find the size of angle L, correct to 1 decimal place.
e: Use the sine rule: $\sin(L)/l = \sin(M)/m$. Rearranging gives $\sin(L) = l \times \sin(M) / m$. So, $\sin(L) = 12 \times \sin(110°) / 18 = 0.6264...$ Angle $L = \arcsin(0.6264...) = 38.79...°$, which is 38.8° to 1 d.p.

q: In triangle XYZ, side x = 5.4 cm, side y = 7.2 cm, and angle Y = 48°. Find the size of angle X, correct to 1 decimal place.
e: Use the sine rule: $\sin(X)/x = \sin(Y)/y$. Rearranging gives $\sin(X) = x \times \sin(Y) / y$. So, $\sin(X) = 5.4 \times \sin(48°) / 7.2 = 0.5573...$ Angle $X = \arcsin(0.5573...) = 33.87...°$, which is 33.9° to 1 d.p.
